$schema-note: Config for `pipeline-cli design-token-guard check` (issue #2170, ADR 0162 / design-system-manifest.md). Read as data by the guard in packages/pipeline-cli; edited from the app side because it grounds against apps/web/src/styles/**. Three bounded, documented allow-lists — see the guard README and the PR body for the model.
externalPropertiesNote: Custom properties a var(--…) ref may resolve to that are NOT declared in any CSS file because they are injected at runtime — by a library (Base UI's Collapsible sets --collapsible-panel-height on the panel) or by our own JS inline style (Controls sets --swatch-color per theme dot). The undefined-ref check treats these as defined. Add a name here only when it is genuinely runtime-injected; a dead ref belongs in grandfatheredMissingTokens.
grandfatheredMissingTokensNote: Pre-existing var(--…) refs to tokens that do NOT exist in the token layer — the exact Toast-class defect (#2167) this rung prevents from recurring, but already present on main and left to the token-recalibration / primitive legs (#2163/#2164/#2166) to resolve. Grandfathered by NAME (not line) so the check still fails on any NEW distinct dead ref. This list only shrinks: a remediation leg that fixes a ref removes its name here.
rawPxCeilingsNote: Per-file ceiling on raw px values > 2px (the 4px grid sanctions 1px & 2px for hairlines/optical nudges — ADR 0162 value #1). A ratchet: a file over its ceiling FAILS (a regression / new raw-px debt), a file NOT listed must be raw-px clean (0), and improvements pass. Regenerate after a cleanup leg with `pipeline-cli design-token-guard check --write-baseline`. This is the bounded, documented allow-list for the ~263 pre-existing raw-px sites the frontend audit catalogued — it never red-walls existing debt but blocks any new bypass of the spacing seam.
